## What this project is
[One-paragraph description of the Angular SPA, the business domain it serves, and which user personas use it.]

## Architecture
- Framework: Angular 17+ with standalone components
- State: [NgRx / Signals API / Component state only]
- Reactive: RxJS 7+ (prefer signals for local state, Observables for async)
- HTTP: `HttpClient` with typed responses and interceptors
- Auth: [MSAL / Keycloak Angular / JWT stored in memory]
- Build: Angular CLI with `strict: true` in tsconfig

## Package structure
```
src/
  app/
    core/           ‚Äî singleton services, interceptors, guards
    shared/         ‚Äî reusable components, pipes, directives
    features/       ‚Äî feature modules (lazy-loaded)
      [feature]/
        components/ ‚Äî standalone components
        services/   ‚Äî feature-scoped services
        store/      ‚Äî NgRx feature store (if applicable)
        models/     ‚Äî TypeScript interfaces / types
    layout/         ‚Äî shell, nav, footer components
```

## Naming conventions
- Components: `PascalCase` + `Component` suffix, standalone by default
- Services: `PascalCase` + `Service` suffix, `providedIn: 'root'` unless feature-scoped
- Interfaces: `PascalCase`, NO `I` prefix (e.g. `User` not `IUser`)
- Enums: `PascalCase` (e.g. `UserRole`)
- Files: `kebab-case.component.ts`, `kebab-case.service.ts`
- Observables: suffix `$` (e.g. `users$: Observable<User[]>`)

## TypeScript standards
- `"strict": true` enforced ‚Äî no `any`, no implicit returns
- Explicit return types on all public methods and functions
- `readonly` on component inputs where mutation is not needed
- Prefer `const` over `let`; never `var`
- Avoid `as` type assertions ‚Äî use type guards instead

## Component rules
- All new components are standalone (`standalone: true`)
- Use `ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush` by default
- Inputs/Outputs via `input()` / `output()` signals (Angular 17+) preferred
- No logic in templates beyond simple conditionals ‚Äî use computed signals or pipes
- Do NOT subscribe in component constructors ‚Äî use `async` pipe or `toSignal()`
- Unsubscribe with `takeUntilDestroyed()` or `DestroyRef`

## API integration
- All HTTP calls go through a typed service, never directly from a component
- API base URLs come from `environment.ts` ‚Äî NEVER hardcoded strings
- Use `HttpErrorResponse` typed error handling in all catch blocks
- Interceptor handles: auth token injection, global error display, request logging

## Test strategy
- Framework: Jasmine + Karma + Angular Testing Library
- Coverage: Istanbul ‚Äî 80% minimum (enforced in CI)
- Unit tests: every service, pipe, and guard has a spec file
- Component tests: test behaviour via user events, not implementation details
- E2E: Cypress with Page Object Model pattern

## PR requirements
- [ ] `ng build --configuration=production` passes with zero warnings
- [ ] Spec file for every new service, component, or pipe
- [ ] No `any` types introduced
- [ ] New routes added to `AppRoutingModule` with lazy loading
- [ ] Accessibility: `aria-*` attributes on interactive elements

## Key eeik_bootstrap agents to use
- `angular-developer` ‚Äî feature components, services, routing
- `angular-tester` ‚Äî Jasmine specs, Cypress E2E tests
- `angular-coverage-checker` ‚Äî Istanbul coverage analysis
- `security-auditor` ‚Äî XSS, CSRF, auth token handling review
- `performance-engineer` ‚Äî bundle size, lazy loading, OnPush strategy

## What Claude should NOT do in this repo
- Do not use `any` type
- Do not subscribe in constructors
- Do not use class-based components without `standalone: true`
- Do not hardcode API URLs
- Do not create services without a corresponding spec file
- Do not use `ngModel` two-way binding on complex forms ‚Äî use ReactiveFormsModule
